evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Build and evolve Data Governance and Data Quality Framework: stale-feed detection, schema validation, range checks, idempotent writes, lineage, ownership, self-healing; Efficiency, quality, idempotency, and observability are taken seriously
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour: governance, quality, idempotency, observability, and self-healing architecture dominate; no velocity/speed language.
evidence.domain_risk: cryptocurrency/trading desks; financial market data; order books; trading; portfolio exposures; regulatory/compliance context
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: High: crypto-trading/fintech platform with explicit financial market data and compliance (risk, compliance teams) signals high domain risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: trading desks; wealth and asset management; product; risk; finance; compliance; research
evidence.data_team_maturity: building the Keyrock Data Platform from the ground up; few months old; you will contribute to most bigger decisions and build work; the standards and way of working are still open
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Early: explicit greenfield platform build; team established only months ago; candidate shapes foundation and standards.
evidence.jd_authorship: Build streaming and batch pipelines; Own data contracts and schema evolution; Design the lakehouse and time-series layer; Build and evolve Data Governance and Data Quality Framework; Build derived analytics; Make observability first-class; Treat infrastructure as code
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Hiring manager: highly specific data engineering ownership (streaming, batch, governance, contracts, analytics layer, infrastructure) shows deep systems expertise.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: trading desks; wealth and asset management; product; risk; finance; compliance; research
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Finance: primary audience is financial/trading/risk teams; role supports trading operations and portfolio analytics.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own data contracts; Design the lakehouse and time-series layer; Build and evolve governance framework; Take ownership end-to-end; Shape standards and way of working
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strategic: candidate sets direction for data platform, owns end-to-end architecture decisions, shapes team standards in greenfield effort.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: JD mentions AI agents as consumers of data platform but not as expected engineer skill.
evidence.testing_framing: Build and evolve Data Governance and Data Quality Framework; schema validation; range checks; idempotent writes; quality taken seriously by default
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Responsibility: data quality and governance ownership framed as core responsibility; validation and quality built into architecture.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Build and evolve Data Governance and Data Quality Framework; stale-feed detection, schema validation, range checks, idempotent writes, lineage, ownership, self-healing; resilient to feed and exchange failures; understand financial market data; compliance context
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: High: governance, quality frameworks, idempotency, resilience, and compliance pervasive; fear is bad data reaching trading desks.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: building the Keyrock Data Platform from the ground up; Build streaming and batch pipelines; Own data contracts; Design the lakehouse and time-series layer
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Greenfield: explicit greenfield platform build from scratch; CDT established only months ago; candidate shapes foundation.
evidence.loss_aversion: Build and evolve the Data Governance and Data Quality Framework: stale-feed detection, schema validation, range checks, idempotent writes, lineage, ownership, self-healing. Resilient to feed and exchange failures.
